D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Mirage — Refractive-Index Gradient Over Hot Road

Hot air n < cool air n; vertical n-gradient bends light, producing inverted images that look like puddles of water on desert sand or asphalt

Inferior mirage: hot air near ground has lower n (≈1.00027 at 60 °C vs 1.00029 at 20 °C). Light rays from distant objects bend upward, then to your eye — the brain projects the image below its real position, creating the illusion of water reflection. Superior mirages (cold sea air) produce floating ships ("Fata Morgana").

Slide ground-temperature contrast.
